title: Damilola Sule
text:
Welcome to my website! I’m Damilola Sule, a Business Analytics graduate student at the Rady School of Management at UC San Diego.
I’m passionate about data, sports, and using analytics to solve real-world problems. As a student-athlete on the UCSD Women’s Basketball Team, I’ve developed strong discipline, teamwork, and leadership skills that carry over into my academic and professional work.

title: Damilola Sule
text:
Welcome to my website! I’m Damilola Sule, a Business Analytics graduate student at the Rady School of Management at UC San Diego.
I’m passionate about data, sports, and using analytics to solve real-world problems. As a student-athlete on the UCSD Women’s Basketball Team, I’ve developed strong discipline, teamwork, and leadership skills that carry over into my academic and professional work.

title: Damilola Sule
text:
About Me
I have a background in Cognitive Behavioral Neuroscience and am currently pursuing my Master’s in Business Analytics. My interests lie in data analysis, marketing analytics, and applying quantitative methods to decision-making.
Through both academic work and hands-on experience, I’ve built skills in Python, SQL, R, and data visualization tools like Tableau and Power BI.

title: Damilola Sule
text:
My Interests


Sports & Health
I play basketball at UCSD and have a strong interest in sports performance, health, and wellness. Athletics has shaped my mindset around discipline, resilience, and teamwork.


Data & Analytics
I enjoy working with data to uncover insights, build models, and support decision-making. I’m especially interested in pricing analytics, marketing analytics, and behavioral data.


Career Goals
My goal is to build a career in analytics where I can combine technical skills with real-world impact — ideally in sports, tech, or business strategy.

A Favorite Quote

title: Projects
text:
Here are some projects that reflect my experience in analytics, data science, and research.



Tools: Python, APIs (Ticketmaster, Spotify), Kaggle

Collected and analyzed event and artist data to identify pricing and demand patterns
Built a regression model to estimate ticket prices based on popularity and timing
Developed a pricing strategy that could generate ~$100K revenue and ~$44K profit




Tools: R, Statistical Modeling

Cleaned and analyzed social media data to evaluate user engagement trends
Built regression models and performed hypothesis testing
Identified key drivers of user behavior and presented insights through visual reports




Tools: Data Processing, Behavioral Analysis

Conducted behavioral experiments using eye-tracking and auditory datasets
Cleaned and structured large datasets for analysis
Presented findings to research teams to support experimental design decisions






Programming: Python, SQL, R, Java, JavaScript, MATLAB :contentReferenceoaicite:0
Tools: Tableau, Power BI, Google Analytics, Excel :contentReferenceoaicite:1
Techniques: Regression, A/B Testing, Data Cleaning, ETL :contentReferenceoaicite:2





Improving my data analysis and modeling skills
Exploring applications of analytics in sports and business
Building projects that combine technical and strategic thinking

title: Sports & Analytics
text:
I am a member of the UCSD Women’s Basketball Team. Being a student-athlete has shaped my mindset around discipline, consistency, and teamwork.
Balancing academics and athletics has helped me develop strong time management skills and resilience.



I am especially interested in how data and analytics can be applied to sports.
This includes: - Player performance analysis - Game strategy and decision-making - Injury prevention and health tracking - Fan engagement and sports business analytics



My background in analytics combined with my experience as an athlete gives me a unique perspective on how data can improve performance both on and off the court.



I am interested in exploring opportunities where I can combine sports and analytics, whether in: - sports analytics - business strategy - or performance optimization
